name: Georgia Peach Pie
recipeIngredient: 8 peaches, peeled and sliced about 2 inches (cut)
recipeInstructions:
Prepare the Crust: In a large bowl, combine the all-purpose flour and salt. Using a pastry blender or two knives, cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Sprinkle 5 to 6 tablespoons of cold water, about 1 tablespoon at a time, over the flour mixture; toss with a fork to blend lightly but evenly. When the mixture forms a ball, gently knead it 2 or 3 times. Divide the dough in half. On a lightly floured surface, roll out one half into a 12-inch circle and fit into a 9-inch pie plate. Trim, leaving a 1-inch overhang. Roll out the remaining half into a 12-inch circle; using a sharp knife, cut it into ten strips about 1 inch wide. Set aside.
Preheat Oven: Heat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Prepare Filling: In a large bowl, combine the peaches, sugar, 1/2 cup flour, lemon juice, and grated lemon peel. Toss well. Pour the filling into the prepared crust. Arrange the reserved strips of dough in a lattice pattern over the filling; trim the strips to match the lower part of the crust's overhang. Lightly dampen the overhang with water. Turn up the ends of the strips and seal, fluting the edge decoratively. Brush the lattice strips and edge of the crust with milk.
Bake: Bake for 50 minutes until bubbling and the crust is golden brown.
